---
title: "Robert W. Walker, Ph. D."
page-layout: full
toc: false
---

I am an [Associate Professor of Quantitative Methods](https://my.willamette.edu/people/rwalker) at the [Atkinson Graduate School of Management](https://willamette.edu/academics/mba/), [Willamette University](https://willamette.edu). My research explores [causal inference](research/causal-inference.qmd), [political economy](research/political-economy.qmd), and statistical computing.  I work to transform complex data into actionable understanding.

::: {.cards}

::: {.card}
## About
Learn about my background, education, and academic journey through appointments at Harvard, Dartmouth, and Texas A&M.

[Learn more →](about.qmd)
:::

::: {.card}
## Research
Causal inference, panel data methods, political economy, and statistical computing with R and Python.

[Explore research →](research.qmd)
:::

::: {.card}
## Publications
Peer-reviewed articles, working papers, and citation metrics from my work in quantitative methods.

[View publications →](publications.qmd)
:::

::: {.card}
## Teaching
Courses in data analytics, statistical methods, and computational social science at Willamette and Essex Summer School.

[See courses →](teaching/index.qmd)
:::

::: {.card}
## Blog
Thoughts on statistics, R programming, data visualization, and reproducible research.

[Read posts →](posts/index.qmd)
:::

::: {.card}
## Contact
Get in touch via email, GitHub, or Twitter. Office hours Wednesdays 1-3pm or by appointment.

[Connect →](contact.qmd)
:::

:::
